entry.title: Road Widening
entry.summary:
The Gulmohor Leafless till now Sprung by orange Blessings on head
Is growing green. Tells shining saw Spare mango tree Cut just me instead

entry.title: The use and abuse of the Uma Devi judgement
entry.summary: In 2006, the Supreme Court ascertained the ‘legality’ of employment in the landmark judgement in Secretary, State of Karnataka v. Umadevi. However, multiple interpretations of the double-edged Uma Devi guidelines have aided the State in shirking off its responsibilities.

entry.title: On thinking and questioning
entry.summary: If a government’s decisions are informed by anything other than scientific thought and reason, it becomes a theocratic government

entry.title: Having captured the legislature and the executive, the judiciary is the final bastion of the Republic that the Hindu Rashtra project is seeking to vanquish
entry.summary: The recent coordinated attack against the Supreme Court reflects the Sangh Parivar’s immense frustration with the sustained and stubborn commitment to the Indian Constitution of an important segment of the judiciary and the Bar

entry.title: Should India revisit the Indus treaty?
entry.summary: Editor’s note: On April 23, 2025, India suspended the Indus Water Treaty with Pakistan, one day after a brutal massacre in the Baisaran valley in Pahalgam claimed the lives of twenty six tourists. We revisit this timely piece, originally published on March 16, 2019

entry.title: An ode to Sanjay Singhvi, for whom life was an unwavering commitment to labour justice
entry.summary: On April 24, senior counsel Sanjay Singhvi passed away. He was among the last in a generation of lawyers who never, for a moment, faltered from their cause.

entry.title: 'Agenda'
entry.summary: Two minutes silence Is duty's response In lieu of mourning For faceless those Never coming back

entry.title: Photo essay: Twelve years after the Rana Plaza tragedy, a collage of unfinished recovery
entry.summary: Much changed regulatorily for the global garment industry after one of the worst labour disasters in contemporary memory killed over 1300 workers in Bangladesh in 2013. But for those who survived there was no truthful rehabilitation

entry.title: The Waqf (Amendment) Act’s insincere promise to India’s Muslim women
entry.summary: The amending legislation’s guarantee of representation for Muslim women in the Central Waqf Council and State Waqf Boards and its progressive vision for women’s inheritance rights are rooted in the idea of treating Muslim women as tokens and diluting entirely the concept of Waqf-ul-Aulad. It is a promise based on insincerity.

entry.title: X Corp. v. Union of India: All you need to know about the Karnataka HC battle over Sahyog portal, censorship and the scope of the IT Act
entry.summary: According to information available with The Leaflet, the matter has been adjourned till 1 July 2025

entry.title: In the US, an assault on science, global health and human rights: Part 2 - Reproductive justice, gender and DEIA under attack
entry.summary: In the second of this three part series special, we delve into the emerging affront to gender diversity, abortion and rights of the LGBTQ+ community in the US under Trump

entry.title: Supreme Court’s indication for deadline on disqualification petitions would reinforce the Tenth Schedule and democratic accountability
entry.summary: Laying down timelines and checking inordinate delays in the Telangana Speaker’s case is a crucial check by the Supreme Court

entry.title: In the US, an assault on science, global health and human rights: Part 1 - The challenges confronting government-led science and health
entry.summary: In the first of this three part series special on the critical challenges to science, health and human rights in the US under the Trump administration, we explore the cascading effects of the closing down of major government projects

entry.title: Collegium recommends transfer of seven High Court judges, taking the tally to 15 transfers under CJI Khanna’s tenure
entry.summary: The latest resolution notes that the transfers are aimed towards better administration of justice and in the interests of inclusivity and diversity

entry.title: SC allows untainted candidates selected for Assistant Teachers for Classes 9 - 12 to continue till fresh recruitment completed: Everything so far in WBSSC recruitment scam case
entry.summary: We bring a complete run-down of the developments in West Bengal’s teacher recruitment scam litigation in the Supreme Court

entry.title: Trump’s policy flip-flop on tariffs indicates he may have a goal, but not so much a plan
entry.summary: President Trump has misdiagnosed the critical problem faced by the US, one rooted in a basic crisis of capitalism and not caused due to higher tariffs byfrom other countries

entry.title: Why the contemptuous tirade by VP Dhankar and BJP MPs against the judiciary is costly for our democracy
entry.summary: While overt closeness between the judiciary and executive is a marker of eroding liberal democratic principles, excessive friction, as seen through recent contemptuous remarks by constitutional functionaries, undermine the judiciary’s integrity before the masses

entry.title: In AMU’s forgotten Kerala campus, students wage a lonely war against maladministration and embezzlement
entry.summary: In Aligarh Muslim University’s Malappuram campus, students uncovered financial irregularities running into lakhs forcing the provost to resign earlier this month. It has been a battle for the Sachar Committee’s promise of educational excellence for India’s marginalised communities.

entry.title: The more they remain the same: Why protests against the new labour codes might be lacking popular grassroots support
entry.summary: Apart from the minimum wages section of the Code of Wages, other labour codes are irrelevant for the vast majority of workers in India

entry.title: Challenge to the Waqf amendment: After two days of hearings, Supreme Court orders that waqf, whether registered or declared by notification, will not be denotified
entry.summary: As the Union files a reply in seven days, Solicitor General Tushar Mehta assured the Court that no new appointments will be made to the Central Waqf Council or State Waqf Board
